title: bupleurum falcatum subsp. eufalcatum
intro: Bupleurum falcatum subsp. eufalcatum, also known as Chinese knotted cudweed or Taiwania, is a subspecies of the Bupleurum falcatum plant, a species of flowering plant in the parsley family. It is a traditional medicinal herb in East Asian medicine, particularly in Chinese medicine, and is valued for its anti-inflammatory and anti-oxidant properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing stress, improving liver function, and boosting the immune system, as well as exhibiting potential anti-cancer and anti-viral properties. Bupleurum falcatum subsp. eufalcatum can be prepared as a tea, decoction, or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs in traditional medicinal formulas.
